claims.claim: When the LFP cell surface temperature reached 182°C, the temperature suddenly dropped about 2°C due to the vent opening of the battery, causing a significant pressure rise from 1.9 to 4.5 bar.
claims.quote: When the cell surface temperature reached 182 ◦C, the temperature suddenly dropped about 2 ◦C because of the vent opening of the battery. This vent opening led to the release of some gases into the canister causing a signiﬁcant pressure rise from 1.9 to 4.5 bar.

claims.claim: For the LFP cell, when the temperature reached above 200°C, thermal runaway occurred, leading to a peak surface temperature of 399°C with a maximum temperature rise rate over 85.5°C/s, and a peak pressure of 31.6 bar with a peak pressure rise rate of 290 bar/s.
claims.quote: When the temperature reached above 200 ◦C, a thermal runaway occurred leading to a very fast temperature and pressure rise. The peak surface temperature reached 399 ◦C with a maximum temperature rise rate over 85.5 ◦C/s. The peak pressure reached 31.6 bar with a peak pressure rise rate of 290 bar/s.

claims.claim: NMC cells had the lowest onset temperatures (140–145°C) and highest peak temperatures (835–998°C), while the LTO cell had a lower onset temperature (163°C) than the LFP cell (200°C) but also a lower peak cell temperature (305°C).
claims.quote: The NMC cells had the lowest onset temperatures and highest peak temperatures, indicating higher thermal instability than the cells with the more stable cathode (LFP) or anode (LTO). Although the LTO cell exhibited a lower onset temperature than the LFP cell, it also had a lower peak cell temperature.

claims.claim: The two NMC cells produced approximately three times the peak pressure of the LFP and LTO cells, with NMC 2 producing the largest pressure of 101.6 bar and the LTO cell having the lowest pressure of 28.8 bar.
claims.quote: The NMC 2 cell produced the largest pressure, 101.6 bar, while the LTO cell had the lowest pressure of 28.8 bar. It is worth noting that the two NMC cells produced about three times the peak pressure of the LFP and LTO cells.

claims.claim: The vented gas volume was approximately proportional to the number of cells when LFP cells were under the same confinement and heating abuse conditions, with one cell producing 3.79 L, two cells producing 8.98 L, and three cells producing 11.72 L.
claims.quote: The experimental data on vented gas volumes for two and three cells indicate that the vented gas volume was approximately proportional to the number of cells when the cells were under the same conﬁnement and heating abuse conditions.

claims.claim: The LFP cell produced the highest levels of H2 (24.34%), C2H2, C2H4, and C2H6 but the lowest CO concentration (4.5%), while NMC cells produced the highest levels of CO (28.06–30.30%) and CH4 (10.50–12.90%) but the lowest levels of C2H4 and C2H6.
claims.quote: The LFP cell produced the highest levels of H2, C2H2, C2H4, and C2H6, but lowest for CO. The NMC cell produced the highest levels of CO and CH4, but the lowest levels of C2H4 and C2H6.

claims.claim: DSC analyses indicated that NMC cell anodes and cathodes were less thermally stable than those for LFP and LTO, with NMC anode exothermic peaks occurring at 159–174°C and NMC cathode exothermic reactions occurring over 125–200°C.
claims.quote: The DSC results indicate that NMC 1 and NMC 2 anodes, and cathodes were less thermally stable than those for LFP and LTO.
